boundary_binding.action_boundary: May AP release payment after the vendor bank update?
boundary_binding.allow_rule_assessment: The chain of records collectively closes all four gates as required by STD-QUA-071-P. Specifically, CALL-QUA-071 provides the pre-change callback, VM-QUA-071 confirms vendor-master approval, BANK-QUA-071 validates bank ownership, and REL-QUA-071 serves as the payment-release authority. All fields, including vendor, amount, account, and release date, match the action boundary.
boundary_binding.controlling_source_fact: The payment-release authority REL-QUA-071, supported by the chain of evidence from CALL-QUA-071, VM-QUA-071, and BANK-QUA-071, confirms all necessary conditions are met.
boundary_binding.escalate_rule_assessment: No narrow source-record defect remains open.
boundary_binding.timing_scope_authority_dependency_check: The action date of 2026-06-28 aligns with the payment-release authority REL-QUA-071, and all supporting documents confirm the vendor (Quanta Field Services), amount (USD 73400), and account (ending 8821) prior to payment.

final_answer: The action may proceed. The chain of source records, including CALL-QUA-071 (pre-change callback), VM-QUA-071 (vendor-master approval), BANK-QUA-071 (bank ownership), and REL-QUA-071 (payment-release authority), collectively satisfies the requirements of STD-QUA-071-P. This explicit articulation of the chain-of-records policy confirms the source boundary is closed, allowing the payment release.
open_blockers: Repairs 041: Make chain-of-records policy explicit in output artifact.
